design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New topic: sharp identified regions for interval regression with several interval-measured regressors under coordinatewise or selected-coordinate monotonicity. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchor is Manski-Tamer interval regression for one interval-measured scalar regressor, with later set-inference work for the resulting identified set. Our theorem is not that because multi-coordinate interval regressors create a partial order: worst-case completions are governed by antichain/frontier geometry rather than the one-dimensional lower/upper endpoint ordering. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an antichain certificate or minimal frontier basis for sharp lower and upper regression-function envelopes, plus a finite two-regressor witness where coordinatewise monotonicity yields a nonrectangular identified region that cannot be recovered by intersecting one-dimensional Manski-Tamer bounds. If the proposal reduces to generic LP duality, ordinary interval arithmetic, or a definition-unfold frontier, pivot or stop early.

proposed_from.topic: New topic: sharp identified regions for interval regression with several interval-measured regressors under coordinatewise or selected-coordinate monotonicity. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchor is Manski-Tamer interval regression for one interval-measured scalar regressor, with later set-inference work for the resulting identified set. Our theorem is not that because multi-coordinate interval regressors create a partial order: worst-case completions are governed by antichain/frontier geometry rather than the one-dimensional lower/upper endpoint ordering. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an antichain certificate or minimal frontier basis for sharp lower and upper regression-function envelopes, plus a finite two-regressor witness where coordinatewise monotonicity yields a nonrectangular identified region that cannot be recovered by intersecting one-dimensional Manski-Tamer bounds. If the proposal reduces to generic LP duality, ordinary interval arithmetic, or a definition-unfold frontier, pivot or stop early.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue entry is manski_nonparametric_bounds_v1, whose headline entries concern bounded-outcome treatment-effect intervals and level-set containment, not latent multivariate interval regressors. The failed manski_tamer_interval_regression_v1 artifact is scalar and explicitly uses lower/upper endpoint classes v1 <= V and v0 >= V. The current qid has no existing proposal artifact, and the net-new object is the finite product-order antichain certificate plus a nonrectangular multivariate witness.","published_axis":"The closest published paper is ManskiTamer2002IntervalRegression, which is scalar interval regression. BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011ConvexMomentPredictions and related random-set papers cover interval covariate data through support functions and convex moment predictions, but do not state a finite coordinatewise monotone antichain frontier for regression-function envelopes. ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013IntersectionBounds is a downstream inference framework once the bound functions are known, not a characterization of those functions."}
seed_list: Introduce minimal antichain certificates for sharp lower and upper multivariate interval-regression envelopes under full coordinatewise monotonicity.
Characterize selected-coordinate monotone interval-regression bounds by conditioning on unrestricted endpoint fibers and using an antichain frontier on selected coordinates.
Prove a finite-support latent-completion theorem that makes multivariate antichain envelopes sharp under one joint monotone regression function.
Construct a two-regressor nonrectangular witness showing coordinatewise monotonicity is not recovered by intersecting scalar Manski-Tamer bounds.
Develop plug-in consistency for estimated antichain/frontier endpoint functionals in multivariate interval regression.
Give a support-function representation of the joint identified region for several target points induced by one product-order monotone completion.
Find the minimal dimension and support size at which scalar rectangular relaxation first fails for interval regression.
Compare random-set support-function bounds with product-order antichain bounds and characterize when the two coincide.
Define a selected-coordinate critical fiber condition under which unrestricted interval coordinates are harmless for monotone envelope sharpness.
Build a finite algorithm for enumerating all minimal lower and upper frontier bases and certifying redundant endpoint cells.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce minimal antichain certificates for sharp lower and upper multivariate interval-regression envelopes under full coordinatewise monotonicity.

anchor_paper.author: Charles F. Manski and Elie Tamer
anchor_paper.claim: Scalar interval-regression bounds use one interval-measured variable and scalar endpoint classes.
anchor_paper.location: Sec. 2 / abstract, p. 330 in indexed abstract; prior proposal Theorem t1
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: interval data are available on one variable

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends scalar to multivariate, but stronger because product intervals create incomparable frontier cells and a minimal certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Beresteanu-Molchanov-Molinari support functions because interval covariates are random sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their support-function framework is broader but does not isolate the finite monotone antichain basis or scalar-collapse frontier.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Manski-Tamer because scalar lower/upper endpoint classes are total orders, while the comparator gap is a product-order antichain certificate.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the focal object is the minimal antichain certificate with a worked two-regressor exhibit, not generic LP duality.

adversarial_referee.instance: Certificate could be promissory without a computed small instance.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Exhibits 9.1 and 9.2 compute the certificate and witness skeleton from endpoint primitives.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sharpness might be assumed through completion regularity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Outcome richness is non-tautological; shared measurable completion remains the conjectured step.

adversarial_referee.instance: Random-set literature may already imply the same bound.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Checklist includes BMM papers; the delta is finite product-order monotone frontier, not abstract support functions.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize selected-coordinate monotone interval-regression bounds by conditioning on unrestricted endpoint fibers and using an antichain frontier on selected coordinates.

anchor_paper.author: Thierry Magnac and Eric Maurin
anchor_paper.claim: Within-interval information changes identified sets for interval-measured latent regressors.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: with point identification only under uniformity within intervals

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens monotonicity, but stronger because projection over unrestricted coordinates is conjectured nonsharp without fiber conditioning.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just applying scalar monotone instrumental-variable bounds inside each coordinate.
seed_details.rebuttal: The natural projection fails because endpoint cells condition on full fibers; the proposal states a necessary fiber condition.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a finite-support latent-completion theorem that makes multivariate antichain envelopes sharp under one joint monotone regression function.

anchor_paper.author: Arie Beresteanu and Francesca Molinari
anchor_paper.claim: Set-valued random variables and Aumann expectations support partial identification with interval data.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: nonconvex cases are handled through convex hulls

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sharpness theorem, stronger if it constructs one shared completion rather than pasting scalar completions independently.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard measurable selection for finite random sets.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial part is isotone completion across incomparable cells under one monotone conditional mean.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a two-regressor nonrectangular witness showing coordinatewise monotonicity is not recovered by intersecting scalar Manski-Tamer bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, and Adam M. Rosen
anchor_paper.claim: Intersection bounds are extrema of bounding-function families, with inference problems after the bounds are specified.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Intersection bounds are determined by the extrema of a collection of bounding functions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample seed, stronger because it targets named scalar Manski-Tamer relaxation and requires a nondegenerate shared-cell witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: A single witness is too hand-tuned to matter.
seed_details.rebuttal: The witness is paired with support-direction incompatibility and can be promoted to an open finite-support class in Stage 0.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not scalar Manski-Tamer because it refutes the rectangular product of scalar relaxations using shared latent product cells.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the object is a comparator-divergence DGP with two support directions, not a generic LP projection.

adversarial_referee.instance: The two-regressor law may be a corner case with engineered cells.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Exhibit 9.2 fixes positive-probability finite cells; Stage 0 must supply nondegenerate numeric means.

adversarial_referee.instance: Scalar Manski-Tamer may not claim rectangular multivariate validity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The comparison is explicitly against coordinatewise scalar relaxation, not against the original paper's theorem.
This remains secondary to the antichain theorem; if unsupported, it downgrades without killing the main seed.

seed_details.one_line: Develop plug-in consistency for estimated antichain/frontier endpoint functionals in multivariate interval regression.

anchor_paper.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Han Hong, and Elie Tamer
anchor_paper.claim: Parameter-set confidence regions apply to moment-inequality/equality models after the set is characterized.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: confidence regions for partially identified models characterized by moment inequalities and equalities

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator extension, weaker because identification frontier must be solved first; useful as follow-on inference.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard intersection-bound inference once envelopes exist.
seed_details.rebuttal: True unless active-antichain estimation changes the index class; keep as field-tier follow-up.

seed_details.one_line: Give a support-function representation of the joint identified region for several target points induced by one product-order monotone completion.

anchor_paper.author: Arie Beresteanu, Ilya Molchanov, and Francesca Molinari
anchor_paper.claim: Convex moment predictions use support functions for interval outcome and covariate data.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: best linear predictions with interval outcome and covariate data

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge seed, stronger if support directions reveal shared-completion restrictions absent from pointwise envelopes.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just BMM support-function machinery with different notation.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only viable if it computes product-order monotone support directions; otherwise drop.

seed_details.one_line: Find the minimal dimension and support size at which scalar rectangular relaxation first fails for interval regression.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Adjacent prior artifact frames nonrectangularity as proper subset of a product region.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: generically nonrectangular: it is a proper subset of the product

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary seed, stronger because it asks for minimal d/support threshold rather than one witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a small combinatorial exercise after the witness is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely true; keep as lower-ranked field-tier support result.

seed_details.one_line: Compare random-set support-function bounds with product-order antichain bounds and characterize when the two coincide.

anchor_paper.author: Arie Beresteanu, Ilya Molchanov, and Francesca Molinari
anchor_paper.claim: Different frameworks require different random-set constructions for observational equivalence.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: different econometric frameworks require different random-set constructions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge seed, stronger if it delivers an iff coincidence condition between support functions and antichain frontiers.
seed_details.referee_objection: This may be a survey-style comparison, not a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only survives if stated as an iff coincidence frontier; not chosen for v1.

seed_details.one_line: Define a selected-coordinate critical fiber condition under which unrestricted interval coordinates are harmless for monotone envelope sharpness.

anchor_paper.author: Charles F. Manski and John V. Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Monotone restrictions sharpen bounds in scalar ordered-instrument settings.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: monotone restrictions can sharpen partial-identification bounds

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Necessary/sufficient condition seed, stronger if it gives a critical fiber condition rather than sufficient monotonicity.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just selected-coordinate weakening of full monotonicity.
seed_details.rebuttal: The fiber condition targets when projection is sharp; otherwise unrestricted coordinates alter feasible placements.

seed_details.one_line: Build a finite algorithm for enumerating all minimal lower and upper frontier bases and certifying redundant endpoint cells.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: The scalar prior uses lower-side and upper-side endpoint classes.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: lies between the supremum ... with v1 <= V and the infimum ... with v0 >= V

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Algorithmic-identifiability seed, weaker unless enumeration is tied to sharpness and certificate minimality.
seed_details.referee_objection: Finite poset enumeration is routine computer science.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; useful as implementation theorem, not the flagship kernel.

proposed_from.literature_map: Manski and Tamer (2002, Econometrica) is the scalar interval-regression anchor: interval data on one variable, scalar monotonicity, mean independence, and simple nonparametric bounds. Manski and Pepper (2000, Econometrica) shows weak monotone restrictions can sharpen partial-identification bounds in scalar ordered settings. Magnac and Maurin (2008, ReStud) shows within-interval distributional information can shrink identified sets. Beresteanu and Molinari (2008, Econometrica) gives random-set/Aumann expectation inference for interval data. Beresteanu, Molchanov, and Molinari (2011, Econometrica) gives support-function/convex-programming tools for interval covariate data but not a finite monotone antichain envelope. Beresteanu, Molchanov, and Molinari (2012, JoE) stresses framework-specific random-set construction. Chernozhukov, Hong, and Tamer (2007, Econometrica) supplies general parameter-set inference after the region is specified. Chernozhukov, Lee, and Rosen (2013, Econometrica) supplies intersection-bound inference and warns against naive analog extrema. The live gap is a product-order antichain/frontier theorem plus a finite nonrectangular two-regressor witness; generic LP duality or scalar interval arithmetic would not meet it.
